markdown:
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 295 **Bulk** L --- You can purchase special armor for animals, called barding (shown on Table 6–18). All animals have a trained proficiency rank in light barding, and combat-trained animals are trained in heavy barding. Barding uses the same rules as armor except for the following. The Price and Bulk of barding depend on the animal’s size. Unlike for a suit of armor, barding’s Strength entry is listed as a modifier, not a score. Barding can’t be etched with magic runes, though special magical barding might be available. ## Barding
**Barding****Size****Price****AC Bonus****Dex Cap****Check Penalty****Speed Penalty****Bulk****Strength**
LightSmall or Medium10 gp+1+5-1-5 ft.2+3
Large20 gp+1+5-1-5 ft.4+3
HeavySmall or Medium (Level 2)25 gp+3+3-3-10 ft.4+5
Large (Level 3)50 gp+3+3-3-10 ft.8+5
 Barding (Light; Small or Medium) **Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 295, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 295 **Price** 10 gp **Bulk** 2 Barding (Light; Large) **Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 295, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 295 **Price** 20 gp **Bulk** 4 Barding (Heavy; Small or Medium) **Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 295, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 295 **Price** 25 gp **Bulk** 4 Barding (Heavy; Large) **Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 295, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 295 **Price** 50 gp **Bulk** 8
name: Barding (Heavy; Large)

markdown:
**Source** [Wake the Dead #5](/Sources.aspx?ID=220) pg. 23 **Price** 90 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand --- This macabre staff is crafted from gleaming white bone, a spinal column spiraling out of its base with a skull swinging from the spine's tip. It was handcraft for Nyctessa by her father, a powerful Blood Lord, from her mortal mother's bones.
When you damage a creature with _Nyctessa’s staff_, that creature gains weakens 2 to negative damage until the end of your next turn. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list.
  • **Cantrip** [_chill touch_](/Spells.aspx?ID=35)
  • **1st** [_animate dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=666), [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=110)
name: Nyctessa's Staff

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Bottled Night](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2780)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Darkness" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=39" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Wake the Dead #5](/Sources.aspx?ID=220) pg. 24

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Tiny black motes swirl inside this crystalline vial. The vial can be thrown up to 20 feet, shattering and releasing the motes to absorb light within a 15-foot burst. Light sources within the motes' area of effect create a 5-foot radius of dim light instead of their normal light and light sources outside the motes to not illuminate the area within them. The darkness lasts for 1 minute, but will disperse immediately in a strong wind.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bottled Night

    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs=""
>
[Plate of Yled](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2781)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=1" />
<trait label="Evil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=64" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Wake the Dead #5](/Sources.aspx?ID=220) pg. 24

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 4
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sinister suit of jet-black _+1 [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)_ was designed to bolster the graveknights of Yled's undead armies, creating unstoppable legions.

The armor gains additional abilities if it is a graveknight's armor. If a graveknight wearing undamaged _Plate of Yled_ is destroyed, the armor rebuilds its body of the course of 1d10 rounds instead of 1d10 days. A living creature who dons the armor is affected by the [graveknight's curse](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=291) immediately instead of 1 hour. 

<document level="2" id="action-2291" />

**Craft Requirements** The metals used to create this armor must include a fragment from a previous graveknight's armor.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs=""
>
[Steadfast Sentinel](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2782)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Wake the Dead #5](/Sources.aspx?ID=220) pg. 25

**Price** 38 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This catalyst is a bird's claw soaked in a mixture of blood and grave dirt. A [_shadow spy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1163) spell empowered by it creates an unusually long-lasting homunculus. Instead of the spell expiring when you next make your daily preparation, you can renew it by expending a spell slot for _shadow spy_ again, maintaining the same homunculus so it can continue its observations. _Steadfast sentinels_ are often used by powerful spellcasters of Geb to covertly monitor their rivals.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Steadfast Sentinel

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Void Salts](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2783)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Wake the Dead #5](/Sources.aspx?ID=220) pg. 25

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sachet is filled with a fine powered that seethes with energy distilled directly from the Negative Energy Plane. When used in casting a [_grim tendrils_](/Spells.aspx?ID=141) spell, _void salts_ allow you to split the area of the spell, creating any number of lines originating from you that total 30 feet in length. A creature that multiple lines pass through is only affected once.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Void Salts

markdown:
**Source** [Wake the Dead #5](/Sources.aspx?ID=220) pg. 25 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact or Strike --- A viscous acidic goo fills this glass vial, specially designed to eat through metallic objects. You can throw the steelscour as an alchemical bomb, target a metal creature or metal object held by a creature. Steelscour has no effect on living or undead flesh, and deals half damage to objects constructed of non-metal materials.
Instead of using steelscour as a bomb, you can pour it on an adjacent unattended metal object with an Interact action, automatically dealing the listed [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) for 5 rounds with not flat check to end the condition. When used this way, steelscour loses the bomb trait. Steelscour (lesser) **Source** [Wake the Dead #5](/Sources.aspx?ID=220) pg. 25 **Price** 3 gp --- It deals 1d8 persistent acid damage that ignores up to 5 hardness from metal. Steelscour (moderate) **Source** [Wake the Dead #5](/Sources.aspx?ID=220) pg. 25 **Price** 9 gp --- You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls. It deals 2d8 persisten acid damage that ignores up to 5 hardness from metal. Steelscour (greater) **Source** [Wake the Dead #5](/Sources.aspx?ID=220) pg. 25 **Price** 215 gp --- You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls. It deals 3d8 persisten acid damage that ignores up to 10 hardness from metal. Steelscour (major) **Source** [Wake the Dead #5](/Sources.aspx?ID=220) pg. 25 **Price** 210 gp --- You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls. It deals 4d8 persisten acid damage that ignores up to 15 hardness from metal.
name: Steelscour

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

---

Magic wards deflect attacks. Increase the armor's item bonus to AC by 1. The armor can be etched with one property rune.

 You can upgrade the _armor potency rune_ already etched on a suit of armor using the normal process for [upgrading items and runes](/Rules.aspx?ID=3157).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Antimagic](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2787)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 226

**Price** 6,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This intricate rune displaces spell energy, granting you a +1 status bonus to saving throws against magical effects. 

**Activate—Antimagic Armor** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A spell targets you or includes you in its area;  **Effect** The armor attempts to counteract the triggering spell with the effect of a 7th-rank [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) spell and a counteract modifier of +26.

**Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Antimagic

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Energy-Resistant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2788)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 226

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These symbols convey protective forces from the Elemental Planes. You gain resistance 5 to [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72). The crafter chooses the damage type when creating the rune. Multiple energy-resistant runes can be etched onto a suit of armor; rather than using only the strongest effect, each must provide resistance to a different damage type. For instance, a _+2 acid-resistant greater fire-resistant breastplate_ would give you acid resistance 5 and fire resistance 10.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fortification](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2789)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 226

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto medium or heavy armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _fortification_ rune wards against the deadliest attacks. Each time you’re critically hit while wearing the etched armor, attempt a DC 17 flat check. On a success, it becomes a normal hit. This property thickens the armor, increasing its Bulk by 1 and the Strength modifier require to reduce its penalties by 1.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Size-Changing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2795)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 227

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This armor can make itself and its wearer quickly change size. 

**Activate—Change Size** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The armor casts your choice of [_enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=102) or [_shrink_](/Spells.aspx?ID=284) on you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Size-Changing

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Winged](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2796)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 227

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This rune is a swirling glyph on the front of the armor. A large pair of transparent, ephemeral wings floats out from the back of the armor. 

**Activate—Take to the Skies** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You trace the rune on the front of the breastplate, and the armor’s ephemeral wings grow tangible, granting you a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=400) of 25 feet or your land Speed, whichever is slower. This effect lasts for 5 minutes or until you [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=74) it. Once the effect ends, the wings disappear completely, reappearing in their ephemeral form 1 hour later. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
Winged
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 227, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 265

**Price** 2,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 19"
pfs="Standard"
>
Winged (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 227, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 265

**Price** 35,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Once activated, the wings remain tangible indefinitely. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=74) the activation if you choose, and you don’t have to wait an hour to activate the rune again.
name: Winged

This rune is a swirling glyph on the front of the armor. A large pair of transparent, ephemeral wings floats out from the back of the armor. 

**Activate—Take to the Skies** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You trace the rune on the front of the breastplate, and the armor’s ephemeral wings grow tangible, granting you a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=400) of 25 feet or your land Speed, whichever is slower. This effect lasts for 5 minutes or until you [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=74) it. Once the effect ends, the wings disappear completely, reappearing in their ephemeral form 1 hour later.

Cold iron armor sickens certain creatures that touch it. A creature with weakness to cold iron (such as most [demons](/Traits.aspx?ID=42) and [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69)) that critically fails an unarmed attack against a creature in cold iron armor becomes [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) 1. A creature with weakness to cold iron is sickened 1 as long as it wears cold iron armor.

Dawnsilver armor is 1 Bulk lighter than normal (or light Bulk if its normal Bulk is 1, with no effect on armor that normally has light Bulk). <title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Elven Chain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2801)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 228

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk**  varies by armor
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Elven chain is a [chain shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=6) made of dawnsilver that glitters in even the faintest light. Because it’s constructed with small, supple rings, it has no check penalty.

 Created by elven artisans employing ancient crafting techniques, elven chain is exceptionally quiet. Unlike other chain shirts—even other dawnsilver chain shirts—elven chain doesn’t have the [noisy](/Traits.aspx?ID=205) trait. This suit of armor can be etched with runes like any other dawnsilver chain shirt.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

</traits>

**Price** 2,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The initial raw materials must include dawnsilver worth at least 3,125 sp.

pfs="Standard"

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magic Armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 229

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk**  varies by armor
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A suit of magic armor is simply a suit of armor or explorer’s clothing etched with fundamental runes. An [armor potency](/Equipment.aspx?ID=278) rune increases the armor’s item bonus to AC, and a [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) rune adds an item bonus to saving throws.

 The Prices here are for all types of armor. You don’t need to adjust the Price from [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4) to [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13) or the like. These armors are made of standard materials, not precious materials such as dawnsilver.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Magic Armor (+1)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 229, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 556

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This armor has a [_+1 armor potency_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=278) rune (increase the item bonus to AC by 1).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

</traits>

</column>

---

This armor has a [_+1 armor potency_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=278) rune (increase the item bonus to AC by 1) and a [_resilient_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) rune (+1 item bonus to saves).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Electric Eelskin](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2805)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 230

**Price** 950 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Shining, slippery eelskin covers the plates of this _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=147) [greater slick](/Equipment.aspx?ID=289) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4)_. The armor gives you the ability to breathe water and grants you a +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) checks to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) and [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks you attempt in the water. 

**Activate—Unleash Charge** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You cast a 2nd-rank [_thunderstrike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1721) with a DC of 29.

**Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of _thunderstrike_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Electric Eelskin

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Impenetrable Scale](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2806)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 230

**Price** 12,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 3
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Scale Mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=45)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Made of overlapping, lustrous black scales of standard-grade adamantine, this _+2 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [fortification](/Equipment.aspx?ID=285) [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=139) [scale mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=8)_ seems to momentarily thicken at the point of impact when hit. Whenever the armor’s fortification rune successfully turns a significant foe’s critical hit into a normal hit, one of the scales on the armor turns violet. You gain resistance to physical damage equal to the number of violet scales, to a maximum of 8.

 At dawn each day, all the violet scales return to normal.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include 1,600 gp of [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=271).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Impenetrable Scale

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lion's Armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2807)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 230

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 3
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Half Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=49)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Various parts of this _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [half plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=49)_ are forged into the shape of a lion’s head. The golden lion heads grant you a commanding presence and seem to actively growl at your enemies, granting you a +2 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7).

The lions on the armor work in tandem with the lion on a _[lion's shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2823)_. If you critically hit with the _lion’s shield_ using Lion Bite, the lions on the armor roar in support of the shield, causing the target of the shield’s Strike to become [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19). 

**Activate—Roar of the Pride** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The lions on your armor roar, attempting to cow your enemies. Attempt a check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) each enemy within 30 feet of you. On a critical success, the target is also [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) for 1 round.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 230 **Usage** worn armor **Bulk** 3 **Base Armor** [Splint Mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=48) --- This suit of _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [fortification](/Equipment.aspx?ID=285) [splint mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=48)_ has a large, green gemstone inset in a prominent location. While wearing the armor, you feel at ease knowing the armor can protect you in even dire circumstances. The gemstone glows with life essence, casting green light as brightly as a torch. You can suppress or resume this light by using a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.
**Activate—Second Chance** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You would die; **Effect** The armor’s gemstone turns gray as it shares life energy with you. The armor casts [_breath of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1456) on you. **Activate—Shielding Light** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Effect** You tap on the gemstone, producing a protective screen of green light. You cast a 5th-rank [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671) spell. As normal with the spell, you can’t cast shield again (using this activation or other means) for 10 minutes if you use it to [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212). Life-Saver Mail **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 230 **Price** 4,000 gp Life-Saver Mail (Greater) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 230 **Price** 52,500 gp --- The armor is _+3 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [greater fortification](/Equipment.aspx?ID=285) [splint mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=48)_. Shielding Light casts a 9th-rank [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671) spell.
name: Life-Saver Mail

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />

This _+1 [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=277) [chain shirt](/Armor.aspx?ID=6)_ has a collar adorned with stitched images of the phases of the moon. You can see in moonlight as though you had low-light vision. 

**Activate—Dim Sight** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You touch the stitched image of the new moon on the armor's collar and suppress the dazzled condition for 1 minute.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include 33 gp of silver.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Moonlit Chain

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tideplate](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2810)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 231

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 4
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Rippling water motifs decorate this simple suit of _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)_. The plate has been altered for underwater use, so it's check penalty doesn't apply to Acrobatics or Athletics checks in water or similar liquids. While wearing the armor, you gain a +2 item bonus to Athletics checks to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39), and you can breathe underwater. 

**Activate—Ride the Waves** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The ripples on the armor begin to undulate, matching the movement of any nearby water. The armor casts [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=371) on you. 

**Activate—Submerge** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127))
 **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You merge with the water for 10 minutes. While merged, you can't move, you can see through the water if it's clear enough, and you can hear what's going on outside of the water. Water typically can't take damage, but if the water you're merged in is subject to electricity damage or an ability or effect that destroys or dries water, you're expelled from the water and take 10d6 damage. [_Control water_](/Spells.aspx?ID=51) expels you without dealing damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tideplate

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dawnsilver Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2814)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 233

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** varies by shield

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

**Base Material** [Dawnsilver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2917)

</column>

---

Dawnsilver shields are 1 Bulk lighter than normal (or light Bulk if their normal Bulk is 1, with no effect on a shield that normally has light Bulk). When used for a shield bash, they're treated as silver weapons.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dawnsilver Buckler (Standard-Grade)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 233

**Price** 400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The shield has Hardness 3, HP 12, and BT 6.

**Craft Requirements** dawn silver worth at least 50 gp

Orichalcum shields' time-bending properties are particularly useful for keeping them intact. The first time each day an orichalcum shield would be destroyed, it's instead left with 1 Hit Point and the broken condition.

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 234 **Price** 670 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 **Base Shield** [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=19) --- A _dragonslayer's shield_ is a steel shield covered with dragonhide from a specific dragon, which visually distinguishes each shield from the others. While raised, this steel shield (Hardness 8, HP 32, BT 16) grants its circumstance bonus to Reflex saves against area effects (as well as to AC, as normal).
While you hold the shield, it also grants you a +2 circumstance bonus to Will saves against a dragon's frightful presence ability. The shield has resistance 10 against the damage type corresponding to the dragon breath of the dragon whose hide was used in its creation (for example, a _dragonslayer's shield_ made with the hide of a diabolic dragon would grant resistance to fire); this applies after reducing the damage for Hardness, so when you use Shield Block, the dragonslayer's shield takes 18 less damage from attacks of that damage type. You can use Shield Block against effects that deal damage of that type. **Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include at least 30 gp of dragonhide.
name: Dragonslayer's Shield

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 234

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** strapped to 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Buckler](/Shields.aspx?ID=17)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _floating shield_ is usually carved with wing motifs. This buckler (Hardness 6, HP 24, BT 12) can protect you on its own. 

**Activate—Float** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The shield magically releases itself and floats off your arm into the air next to you, granting you its bonus automatically, as if you had Raised the Shield. Because you're not wielding the shield, you can't use reactions such as Shield Block with the shield, but you gain its benefits even when using both of your hands. After 1 minute, the shield drops to the ground, ending its floating effect. While the shield is adjacent to you, you can Interact to grasp it, ending its floating effect early.

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 234 **Price** 975 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 **Base Shield** [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=19) --- The religious symbol of Torag, the forge god—an ornate hammer of dwarven construction—adorns the face of this _lesser reinforcing steel shield_ (Hardness 8, HP 72, BT 36). The shield is a religious symbol of Torag.
You and any adjacent allies have fire resistance 5 while you have the shield raised. When used for a [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839), the _forge warden_ rings out like the hammer strike of a blacksmith, and the symbol glows as if lit by the fires of a furnace. **Activate—Forge Fire** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Trigger** You use the _forge warden_ to Shield Block an adjacent creature's attack, and the shield takes damage; **Effect** The attacking creature takes 2d6 fire damage.
name: Forge Warden

An indestructible shield is a _high-grade greater reinforcing adamantine shield_ (Hardness 17, HP 136) that can withstand just about any damage. It can be damaged only by a [disintegrate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1492) spell (roll damage as if against a creature that failed its save) or by an artifact tied to destruction.

**Craft Requirements** The raw materials must include at least 4,400 gp of adamantine.

This steel shield (Hardness 6, HP 36, BT 18) is forged into the shape of a roaring lion's head. The lion's head functions as _+1 striking_ [shield boss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=41) that can't be removed from the shield. 

**Activate—Lion's Bite** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** Your Shield is raised; **Effect** You animate the lion's head, making a melee Strike with it. The shield's biting maw is a martial melee weapon that deals 2d6 piercing damage and has the [deadly](/Traits.aspx?ID=174) d6 trait; it can't be enhanced by runes. The shield remains animated for 1 minute, during which time you can Strike with it each time you [Raise the Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=98), as well as with a Strike action.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 234

**Price** 18,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Buckler](/Shields.aspx?ID=17)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This high-grade silver buckler (Hardness 6, HP 24, BT 12) is polished to a mirrorlike sheen. The shield functions as a [spellguard shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=325) that can also reflect spells. 

**Activate—Reflect Spell** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You're targetd by a spell; **Requirements** The _reflecting shield is raised_; **Effect** You attempt to reflect the spell on its caster. The shield attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the spell, with a counteract rank of 9th and a counteract modifier of +30. If the spell is successfully counteracted, it's turned back on its caster. 

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include at least 2,750 gp of silver.

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 234 **Price** 360 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 **Base Shield** [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=19) --- Five jagged spines project from the surface of this steel shield (Hardness 6, HP 24, BT 12). The spines are _+1 striking_ [shield spikes](/Weapons.aspx?ID=42). When you use the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) reaction with this shield, the spines take the damage before the shield itself does. When the shield would take damage (after applying Hardness), one spine snaps off per 6 damage, reducing the damage by 6. The shield takes any remaining damage. When there are no spines left, the shield takes damage as normal.
When all the spines are gone, you lose the ability to attack with them until the spines regenerate the next day. **Activate—Fire Spine** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Effect** You shoot one of the shield's spines at a target. A fired spine uses the spikes' statistics, but it's a martial ranged weapon with a range increment of 120 feet.
name: Spined Shield

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 236 **Usage** etched onto a weapon --- A _striking rune_ stores destructive magic in the weapon, increasing the weapon damage dice it deals to two instead of one. For instance, a _+1 striking [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)_ would deal 2d4 damage instead of 1d4 damage.
You can upgrade the _striking rune_ already etched on a weapon to a stronger version, increasing the values of the existing rune to those of the new rune. The Price of the upgrade is the difference between the two runes' Prices. Striking **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 236, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 581 **Price** 65 gp Striking (Greater) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 236, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 581 **Price** 1,065 gp --- The weapon deals three weapon damage dice. Striking (Major) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 236, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 581 **Price** 31,065 gp --- The weapon deals four weapon damage dice.
name: Striking

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 236 **Usage** etched onto a weapon --- Magical enhancements make this weapon strike true. Attack rolls with this weapon gain a +1 item bonus, and the weapon can be etched with one property rune.
You can upgrade the _weapon potency_ rune already etched on a weapon to a stronger version, increasing the values of the existing rune to those of the new rune. The Price of the upgrade is the difference between the two runes' prices. Weapon Potency (+1) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 236, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 581 **Price** 35 gp **Craft Requirements** You are an expert in Crafting. Weapon Potency (+2) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 236, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 581 **Price** 935 gp --- The item bonus to attack rolls is +2, and the weapon can be etched with two property runes. **Craft Requirements** You are a master in Crafting. Weapon Potency (+3) **Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 236, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 581 **Price** 8,935 gp --- The item bonus to attack rolls is +3, and the weapon can be etched with three property runes. **Craft Requirements** You are legendary in Crafting.
name: Weapon Potency

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 236 **Price** 2,700 gp **Usage** etched onto a melee weapon --- An _animated weapon_ flies autonomously and strikes your foes. **Activate—Set Free** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Effect** You Release the weapon and it flutters through the air, fighting on its own against the last enemy you attacked, or the nearest enemy to it if your target has been defeated. At the end of your turn each round, the weapon can [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) up to its fly Speed of 40 feet, and then can either Fly again or Strike one creature within its reach.
The weapon has a space of 5 feet, but it doesn't block or impede enemies attempting to move though that space, nor does it benefit from or provide flanking. The weapon can't move through an enemy's space. The weapon can't use reactions, and its Fly actions don't trigger reactions.
While it's activated, an _animated_ weapon makes Strikes with an attack modifier of +24 plus its item bonus to attack rolls. It uses the weapon's normal damage but has a +0 Strength modifier. The weapon's abilities that automatically trigger on a hit or critical hit still function, but the weapon can't be activated or benefit from any of your abilities while animated.
Each round, when the weapon finishes using its actions, attempt a DC 6 flat check. On a failure, the activation ends. The weapon falls to the ground and can't be Set Free again for 10 minutes.
name: Animated

<traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 236

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Astral_ weapons command powerful spiritual energy from the Astral Plane. This rune has the same effects as a [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) rune, plus Strikes with it deal an extra 1d6 spirit damage. If used to attack a creature that's possessing another creature, this weapon deals no damage to the possessed creature. On a critical hit against a creature possessing another creature, the possessing creature must succeed at a DC 26 Will save or be expelled and unable to possess a creature for 1d4 rounds.

</title>

<traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 236

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Astral_ weapons command powerful spiritual energy from the Astral Plane. This rune has the same effects as a [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) rune, plus Strikes with it deal an extra 1d6 spirit damage. If used to attack a creature that's possessing another creature, this weapon deals no damage to the possessed creature. On a critical hit against a creature possessing another creature, the possessing creature must succeed at a DC 26 Will save or be expelled and unable to possess a creature for 1d4 rounds.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Brilliant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2833)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 236

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This rune causes a weapon to transform into pure, brilliant energy. The weapon deals an additional 1d4 fire damage on a successful Strike, as well as 1d4 spirit damage to fiends and 1d4 vitality damage to undead. On a critical hit, the target must succeed at a DC 29 Fortitude save or be blinded for 1 round. 

**Activate—Shine Bright** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100))    **Effect** You plunge your weapon into darkness to return the light. Attempt a counteract check with a counteract rank of 5 and a +19 counteract modifier to end a magical darkness effect whose area is within reach of the weapon.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Brilliant
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 236, [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 180

**Price** 2,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Brilliant (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 236, [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 180

**Price** 24,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The save DC is 41, the counteract rank is 9, the counteract modifier is +31, and damage dealt by this weapon ignores the target's resistances to fire, spirit, and vitality
name: Brilliant

[Corrosive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2834)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Acid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=524" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 237

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Acid sizzles across the surface of the weapon etched with this rune. When you hit with the weapon, add 1d6 acid damage to the damage dealt. In addition, on a critical hit, the target's armor (if any) takes 3d6 acid damage (before applying Hardness); if the target has a shield raised, the shield takes this damage instead.

</column>

Corrosive

right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Corrosive (Greater)
</title>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 237, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 583

**Price** 6,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The acid damage dealt by this weapon ignores the target's acid resistance. Increase the acid damage dealt to armor or a shield on a critical hit to 6d6.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Decaying](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2835)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Void" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=510" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 237

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Eerie waves of purplish energy dance across the surface of a weapon etched with this rune. When you hit with the weapon, add 1d4 void damage to the damage dealt. In addition, on a critical hit, the target takes 2d4 [persistent void damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29); if the target has a shield raised, the shield takes the same amount of persistent damage (its wielder rolls the flat check to see if the persistent damage ends, or the GM rolls if the shield is no longer in someone's possession). Unlike normal void damage, the void damage from a decaying rune damages objects, constructs, and the like by eroding them away.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Decaying](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2835)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Void" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=510" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 237

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Eerie waves of purplish energy dance across the surface of a weapon etched with this rune. When you hit with the weapon, add 1d4 void damage to the damage dealt. In addition, on a critical hit, the target takes 2d4 [persistent void damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29); if the target has a shield raised, the shield takes the same amount of persistent damage (its wielder rolls the flat check to see if the persistent damage ends, or the GM rolls if the shield is no longer in someone's possession). Unlike normal void damage, the void damage from a decaying rune damages objects, constructs, and the like by eroding them away.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

A weapon etched with this rune can harm creatures without physical form. A _ghost touch_ weapon is particularly effective against [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures, which almost always have a specific vulnerability to ghost touch weapons. Incorporeal creatures can touch, hold, and wield ghost touch weapons (unlike most physical objects).

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 238 **Price** 700 gp **Usage** etched onto a weapon --- When your attack roll with this weapon is a critical hit and gains the critical specialization effect, you gain an additional benefit depending on the weapon group. **[Axe](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1)** You can damage a third creature, with the same restrictions.
**[Bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3)** The Athletics check to pull the missile free is DC 20.
**[Brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4)** The target takes a –4 circumstance penalty to its save.
**[Club](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=5)** You can knock the target up to 15 feet away.
**[Crossbow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=17)** If the target of the critical hit is adjacent to a surface, it gets stuck to that surface by the missile, as the [bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3) critical specialization.
**[Dart](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=6)** The base persistent bleed damage increases to 2d6.
**[Firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16)** The target takes a –4 circumstance penalty to its save.
**[Flail](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=7)** You move the target 5 feet. You can't move it away from you, but you can move it in another direction of your choice.
**[Hammer](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=8)** You can also knock the target 5 feet away from you.
**[Knife](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=9)** The target takes a –5-foot status penalty to its Speed while it has the persistent bleed damage.
**[Pick](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=10)** The extra damage from the critical specialization effect increases to 4 per weapon damage die.
**[Polearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=11)** You can move the target up to 10 feet.
**[Shield](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=12)** You can knock the target up to 10 feet away.
**[Sling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=13)** The target also takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speed for 1 round if it fails the save.
**[Spear](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14)** The [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) condition lasts for 2 rounds.
**[Sword](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=15)** The target is off-guard until the end of your next turn.
name: Grievous

A _holy_ weapon commands powerful celestial energy. Strikes made with it gain the holy trait and deal an extra 1d4 spirit damage, or an extra 2d4 against an unholy target. If you are unholy, you are enfeebled 2 while carrying or wielding this weapon. 

**Activate—Holy Healing** <actions string="Reaction" /> [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You critically succeed at a Strike against an unholy creature with the weapon;  **Effect** You regain HP equal to double the unholy creature's level

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 238 **Price** 225 gp **Usage** etched onto a melee weapon --- With a moment of manipulation, you can shift this weapon into a different weapon with a similar form. **Activate—Shift Weapon** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Effect** The weapon takes the shape of another melee weapon that requires the same number of hands to wield. The weapon's runes and any precious material it's made of apply to the weapon's new shape. Any property runes that can't apply to the new form are suppressed until the item takes a shape to which they can apply.
The weapons a _shifting_ weapon can turn into are based on the base attributes of the weapon, so reference the weapon's Hands entry in the weapons table to see what it can turn into. For example, a bastard sword requires one hand, even though it gets a benefit in two hands from the two-hand trait. Therefore, a _shifting bastard sword_ could turn into a longsword, but not a greatsword. Activating this rune doesn't change how many hands you're currently using to hold the weapon.
name: Shifting

**Usage** etched onto a bludgeoning melee weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Shockwave_ weapons magically increase their density and momentum when swung, creating a thudding impact on those nearby. Strikes with this weapon deal bludgeoning splash damage equal to the number of weapon damage dice. You're immune to this splash damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shockwave

markdown:
**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 239 **Price** 2,700 gp **Usage** etched onto a melee weapon --- A _spell reservoir_ rune creates a pool of eldritch energy within the etched weapon. A spellcaster can spend 1 minute to cast a spell of 3rd rank or lower into the weapon. The spell must require 2 actions or fewer to cast and must be able to target a creature other than the caster. The spell has no immediate effect—it is instead stored for later.
When you wield a _spell reservoir_ weapon, you immediately know the name and rank of the stored spell. A spell reservoir weapon found as treasure has a 50% chance of having a spell of the GM's choice stored in it. **Activate—Channeled Release** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Requirements** A spell is stored in the weapon; **Effect** Make a Strike with the weapon. You expend the stored spell as part of this Strike; this empties the spell from the weapon and allows a spell to be cast into it again. If the Strike hits, the spell targets the target of the attack. If the spell requires a spell attack roll, the result of your attack roll with the weapon determines the degree of success of the spell, and if the spell requires a saving throw, the DC is 30. **Activate—Safe Release** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Effect** Harmlessly expend the stored spell. This frees the weapon to have a new spell cast into it.
name: Spell Reservoir

    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Thundering](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2850)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Sonic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=697" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 239

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _thundering_ weapon lets out a peal of thunder when it hits, dealing an extra 1d6 sonic damage on a successful Strike. On a critical hit, the target must succeed at a DC 24 Fortitude save or be deafened for 1 minute (or 1 hour on a critical failure).   

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thundering
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

</row>

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 238, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 585

**Price** 6,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The save DC is 34, and the deafness is permanent. Sonic damage dealt by this weapon ignores the target's sonic resistance.
name: Thundering

    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Unholy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2851)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 239

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched into a weapon that isn't holy

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An unholy rune instills fiendish power into the etched weapon. Strikes made with it gain the unholy trait and deal an extra 1d4 spirit damage, or an extra 2d4 against a holy target. If you are holy, you are enfeebled 2 while carrying or wielding this weapon.

**Activate—Unholy Bloodshed** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You critically succeed at an attack
roll against a holy creature with the weapon;  **Effect** The target takes persistent bleed damage equal to 1d8 per weapon damage die of the etched weapon.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Unholy
